prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's trust signals.

Locksmiths have a massive trust problem — the industry is plagued by scam operators. Trust signals are critical for conversion.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No reviews or testimonials on site
- No mention of license or insurance
- No trade body membership (MLA, ALOA)
- No guarantee mentioned
- Stock photos of locks instead of real team
- No Google Reviews integration

COLD LEAD signals:
- Embedded Google/Trustpilot reviews
- Trade body logos (Master Locksmiths Association)
- License and insurance numbers
- Named team members with photos
- Guarantee/warranty stated
- Case studies or before/after photos
- Years in business prominently displayed

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of trust signals>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = fewer trust signals = better lead>",
  "hasReviews": true/false,
  "hasTradeBody": true/false,
  "hasInsurance": true/false,
  "hasNamedTeam": true/false,
  "hasGuarantee": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://londonlocksmiths.com/about/",
      "pageName": "London Locksmiths – About",
      "summary": "Strong trust signals overall: named team members with detailed bios, MLA membership prominently stated, and 35 years of experience highlighted. However, stock Adobe photos are used instead of real team images, and there is no visible mention of reviews, guarantees, or insurance details.",
      "opportunityScore": 3,
      "hasReviews": false,
      "hasTradeBody": true,
      "hasInsurance": false,
      "hasNamedTeam": true,
      "hasGuarantee": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Add embedded Google Reviews or Trustpilot widget and real team photography to replace stock Adobe images — the strong MLA and bio content deserves authentic visuals to maximise conversion.",
      "notes": "This is a well-established business with genuine credentials but a cold digital presence; a review integration and photo upgrade would be a relatively easy sell with clear ROI framing around trust and conversion."
    }
  ]
}
